name: Quadra
description: Quad analog function generator. Each channel runs AD, ASR, or Cycle mode with per-channel exponential/linear switch. Optional Quadra Expander adds EOC outs, CV over A/D, analog OR logic outs and Quadrature mode.

controls.name: ATTACK
controls.description: Attack time — rise from zero to peak. CCW = fastest. Fastest times achieved in linear mode.

controls.name: DECAY
controls.description: Decay/release time. In AD mode = fall-to-zero · in ASR = release after gate · in Cycle = sum with Attack sets LFO rate.

controls.name: Exp / Linear switch
controls.description: Selects curve shape. Linear = straight ramps · Exponential = classic percussive curve. Linear mode permits fastest envelope times.

controls.name: Mode switch (AD / ASR / Cycle)
controls.description: AD = one-shot on trigger (ignores gate length). ASR = attack/sustain/release following the gate. Cycle = AD loops as unipolar LFO.

controls.name: Amplitude LED
controls.description: Visual indicator of each channel's current envelope level.

controls.name: A CV attenuator (Expander)
controls.description: Attenuates the Attack CV input for the channel. Normalized to the D CV attenuator so one CV can control both.

controls.name: D CV attenuator (Expander)
controls.description: Attenuates the Decay CV input for the channel.

controls.name: QUAD 1+2 switch (Expander)
controls.description: Engages Quadrature mode for 1+2. CH 1's end-of-attack triggers CH 2. Creates interdependent looping envelopes that resemble 90°-shifted waveforms.

controls.name: OR amount (ENV 2 / ENV 3)
controls.description: Sets how much of ENV 2 (or ENV 3) is summed into the OR 1+2 (or OR 3+4) output. Used to dial sustain level when constructing ADSR from AD+ASR pairs.

inputs.name: TRIG 1–4
inputs.description: Gate/trigger input per channel. Triggers AD, gates ASR, resets/syncs Cycle.

inputs.name: A CV 1–4 (Expander)
inputs.description: Voltage control of attack time. Normalized to D CV jack of same channel.

outputs.name: EOC 1–4 (Expander)
outputs.description: End-of-Cycle gate — high when the channel completes its decay phase

outputs.name: OR 1+2 (Expander)
outputs.description: Analog OR (peak) of ENV 1 and ENV 2 (ENV 2 scaled by OR amount). Used to fabricate ADSR shapes.

outputs.name: OR 3+4 (Expander)
outputs.description: Analog OR (peak) of ENV 3 and ENV 4 (ENV 3 scaled by OR amount)

behaviors.name: AD mode
behaviors.description: One-shot AD envelope, completes full cycle regardless of gate length. Ideal for sequencer-driven percussive and pluck envelopes.

behaviors.name: ASR mode
behaviors.description: Attack on gate rise, sustains at peak while gate is high, releases on gate fall. Used for keyboard-style voices where note length matters.

behaviors.name: Cycle mode
behaviors.description: AD loops continuously as a unipolar LFO. Total rate = Attack + Decay times. No trigger required; TRIG input still resets phase.

behaviors.name: ADSR via OR output
behaviors.trigger: CH 1 = AD · CH 2 = ASR · both triggered by same gate → OR 1+2
behaviors.description: Triggering an AD and ASR channel simultaneously produces an ADSR shape at the OR output. ENV 2 OR attenuator sets sustain level. Fabricates classic ADSR from two Quadra channels.

behaviors.name: Quadrature mode
behaviors.description: CH 1 self-cycles via its own EOC. CH 2 triggers on CH 1's end-of-attack but won't decay until CH 1 finishes its decay. Creates phase-shifted looping envelopes; great for OR-mixed complex LFO shapes.

behaviors.name: A/D CV normal
behaviors.description: Attack CV jack is normalized to Decay CV — patching to A CV only modulates both attack and decay together. Patching D CV breaks the normal for independent control.

behaviors.name: Linear mode speeds up
behaviors.description: Linear curves permit the fastest envelope times. Exponential mode produces classic VCA-friendly percussive curves but has a speed floor.

patchIdeas.name: Classic ADSR keyboard voice
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 = AD · CH 2 = ASR · both triggered by keyboard gate · ENV 2 OR attenuator sets sustain · OR 1+2 → VCA CV · one-knob sustain control with true ADSR shape
patchIdeas.diagram:
Quadra:
* CH 1: AD mode · linear
* CH 2: ASR mode · linear
* Expander: ENV 2 OR atten = sustain level

Keyboard:

- Keyboard (Gate) g> Quadra (TRIG 1)
- Keyboard (Gate) g> Quadra (TRIG 2)
- Quadra Expander (OR 1+2) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Kick drum (West Coast)
patchIdeas.patch: Sine VCO → VCA 2 · CH 1 AD (fast A, med D) → VCO pitch CV via attenuator (pitch sweep) · CH 2 AD (fast A, slightly shorter D) → VCA CV (amp) · both triggered from sequencer · snappy boomy kick
patchIdeas.diagram:
Quadra:
* CH 1: AD · exp · fast A, medium D
* CH 2: AD · exp · pitch decay < amp decay

VCO:

VCA:

Attenuator:

Sequencer:

- Sequencer (Trig) t> Quadra (TRIG 1)
- Sequencer (Trig) t> Quadra (TRIG 2)
- Quadra (ENV 1) >> Attenuator (In)
- Attenuator (Out) p> VCO (FM)
- VCO (Sine) -> VCA (In)
- Quadra (ENV 2) >> VCA (CV)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Quadrature LFO pair
patchIdeas.patch: QUAD 1+2 switch ON · CH 1 + CH 2 in AD mode, short A and D · OR 1+2 → complex looping LFO shape · ENV 1 and ENV 2 individually drive two filter cutoffs for stereo motion that never phases together identically
patchIdeas.diagram:
Quadra:
* CH 1: AD · CH 2: AD
* Expander: QUAD 1+2 ON

- Quadra (ENV 1) >> Filter L (Cutoff)
- Quadra (ENV 2) >> Filter R (Cutoff)
- Quadra Expander (OR 1+2) >> VCA (CV) // complex composite shape

patchIdeas.name: Chained four-stage envelope
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 AD · EOC 1 → TRIG 2 · CH 2 AD · EOC 2 → TRIG 3 · continue through CH 4 · single trigger fires four sequential envelopes to four destinations (filter, VCA, pan, wavefolder)
patchIdeas.diagram:
Quadra:
* CH 1-4: AD

Sequencer:

- Sequencer (Trig) t> Quadra (TRIG 1)
- Quadra Expander (EOC 1) t> Quadra (TRIG 2)
- Quadra Expander (EOC 2) t> Quadra (TRIG 3)
- Quadra Expander (EOC 3) t> Quadra (TRIG 4)
- Quadra (ENV 1) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Quadra (ENV 2) >> VCA (CV)
- Quadra (ENV 3) >> Pan (CV)
- Quadra (ENV 4) >> Wavefolder (Fold)

patchIdeas.name: Self-cycling kick with ducking
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 Cycle mode (A+D = tempo) = tempo clock · CH 2 AD fires envelope on each EOC 1 pulse · CH 2 → inverter → bass VCA CV for sidechain ducking against the self-cycling kick on CH 1
patchIdeas.diagram:
Quadra:
* CH 1: CYCLE · A+D = tempo
* CH 2: AD · longer D

- Quadra Expander (EOC 1) t> Quadra (TRIG 2)
- Quadra (ENV 1) >> Kick VCA (CV)
- Quadra (ENV 2) >> Inverter (In)
- Inverter (Out) >> Bass VCA (CV)
- Kick VCO (Out) -> Kick VCA (In)
- Bass VCO (Out) -> Bass VCA (In)

patchIdeas.name: Internal A/D CV sweep
patchIdeas.patch: CH 3 Cycle as slow LFO · ENV 3 → CH 1 A CV (Expander · unpatched D CV normals through) · as LFO rises, both attack and decay of CH 1 lengthen together — envelope on CH 1 gets progressively lazier over minutes
patchIdeas.diagram:
Quadra:
* CH 1: AD · CH 3: CYCLE (very slow)

Sequencer:

- Sequencer (Trig) t> Quadra (TRIG 1)
- Quadra (ENV 3) >> Quadra Expander (A CV 1) // D CV normals from A CV
- Quadra (ENV 1) >> VCA (CV)
